q: Biology is broadly divided into five major themes: organization, information, energy and matter, interactions and ___.
Life on Earth is simply amazing — diverse, ___, powerful, intelligent, and mystify.
In the time, that humans have studied biology understanding of its five major themes has been able to be ___.
The hierarchy of life on earth is unfolded as follows: biosphere, ecosystem, community, population, organism, organ system, organ, tissue, cell, ___, molecule and atom.
With each set up, new properties emerge (___) because of interactions among components at the lower levels.
At each level of the biological hierarchy, a correlation of ___ and function can be observed.
The ___, one of the steps composed in the hierarchy of life is a basic unit structure and function of an organism.
Cells are either ___ or eukaryote.
Eukaryotic cells contain ___, including DNA in nucleus whereas prokaryotic cells lack them.
The genetic information is encoded in the ___ sequences of DNA.
DNA sequences (called ___) act as instructions to make a cell’s protein production.
DNA sequences are transcribed into ___ and then translated into specific proteins.
The process of protein production from DNA is called ___.
Energy flows through the ___.
___ convert energy from sunlight to chemical energy.
Chemical cycle occurs between ___ and the environment.
Organisms interact continuously with ___.
In ___, accumulation of the product slows its production.
In ___, a product speeds up its own production.
___ is a type of regulation common to life at all levels, from molecules to ecosystems.
___ is the process of change that has transformed life on Earth.
Biologists classify species according to a system of ___ and broader groups.
Domain ___ and Archaea consist of prokaryotes.
Domain Eukarya includes various groups of ___ as well as fungi, plants, and animals.
Darwin proposed ___ as the mechanism for evolutionary adaptation of populations to their environments.
Natural selection is the evolutionary process that occurs when a population is exposed to ___.
